steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: "Having proportionally large populations" is a gerund phrase functioning as the grammatical subject of the clause. A gerund phrase is treated as singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a gerund phrase as subject takes a singular verb.
  • Choice A — "are": plural present verb; gerund subjects take singular verbs, so "are" is incorrect. ✗
  • Choice B — "were": plural past verb; wrong in number and tense — the sentence describes a general ongoing truth. ✗
  • Choice C — "have been": plural present perfect; wrong in number for a singular gerund subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "is": singular present verb; correctly agrees with the singular gerund-phrase subject. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
